name: broken_chain_integrity
description: A peer that produces blocks normally until slot 115, then substitutes a wrong prev_hash from slot 115 onward. The individual blocks parse correctly and the header CBOR is syntactically valid, but the hash chain is broken: block at slot 115 claims a previous block that doesn't match what was actually produced at slot 110. Tests whether the client-under-test validates hash-chain continuity during chain-sync. If it does, it should error at slot 115. If it doesn't, it will silently accept the broken chain — either is a real conformance finding.

peers.description: Produces honest blocks until slot 110; from slot 115 prev_hash is deliberately wrong
